contents.title: 6 easy steps for starting international trade 2021 (import/export)

vocabulary.definition: Goods or services brought into a country from another country.
examples: The country's imports of oil have increased.
Imports are essential for many industries.
vocabulary.context_sentence: Imports are any good or service brought in from one country to another

vocabulary.definition: Goods or services produced in one country and sold to another.
examples: The country's exports include manufactured goods and agricultural products.
Increased exports can boost a country's economy.
vocabulary.context_sentence: while exports are goods and services produced in the home country for sale to other markets

vocabulary.definition: Involving many different and connected parts; intricate.
examples: The instructions were complex and difficult to understand.
The global economy is a complex system.
vocabulary.context_sentence: The modern system of international trade is a complex web of import/export businesses that

vocabulary.definition: The action of sharing something out among a number of recipients.
examples: The company handles the distribution of its products.
Efficient distribution is crucial for success.
vocabulary.context_sentence: handle the sale, distribution, and delivery of goods from one nation to another

vocabulary.definition: The quality of being trusted and believed in.
examples: The company needs to build its credibility.
His credibility was damaged by the scandal.
vocabulary.context_sentence: it is all about proving that your organization is trustable

vocabulary.definition: Made lawful; given legal status.
examples: The government legalized the use of cannabis.
The company's activities must be legalized to operate.
vocabulary.context_sentence: in order to get any certification any license to make your activities legalized

vocabulary.definition: Having or showing strong feelings or enthusiasm for something.
examples: She is passionate about environmental issues.
He is passionate about his work.
vocabulary.context_sentence: well, find a product or industry you are passionate about and that you think could sell in international markets

vocabulary.definition: To recognize or establish; to determine.
examples: The police are trying to identify the suspect.
We need to identify the problem before we can solve it.
vocabulary.context_sentence: after finding the right product, identify the right market

vocabulary.definition: Of great importance; essential.
examples: This decision is crucial to our success.
A good education is crucial for a successful career.
vocabulary.context_sentence: A strong relationship with a manufacturer/supplier is crucial to long-running success in an imports/exports business.

vocabulary.definition: The detailed organization and implementation of a complex operation.
examples: The company has a strong logistics department.
The logistics of the project were very challenging.
vocabulary.context_sentence: and figure out the logistics of taking their product from their local warehouse to another one, maybe other side of the globe

vocabulary.definition: The amount added to the cost price of goods to cover overhead and profit.
examples: The retailer added a 50% markup to the product's cost.
The company needs to adjust its markup to remain competitive.
vocabulary.context_sentence: Be sure to price your product such that your markup wont exceed what a customer is willing to pay

vocabulary.definition: A person or company that supplies goods to retailers.
examples: The company uses distributors to sell its products.
Finding reliable distributors is key to market penetration.
vocabulary.context_sentence: You usually need to find distributors and clients who will take on your product and sell to others

vocabulary.definition: The act of organizing people or things so that they work together effectively.
examples: The project required a high degree of coordination.
Effective coordination is essential for success.
vocabulary.context_sentence: it requires an extraordinary amount of coordination

vocabulary.definition: A company that organizes shipments for individuals or corporations to get goods from the manufacturer or producer to a market, customer or final point of distribution.
examples: The company hired a freight forwarder to handle the shipping.
A freight forwarder can simplify the import/export process.
vocabulary.context_sentence: Hiring a global freight forwarder is generally a good idea for all imports/exports businesses,

grammar.title: Passive Voice
grammar.explanation: Used to describe actions where the subject is acted upon. Useful for describing processes and avoiding personal pronouns.
examples: The product is sold internationally.
The goods were delivered on time.
grammar.context_sentence: exports are goods and services produced in the home country for sale to other markets

grammar.title: Present Perfect
grammar.explanation: Used to describe actions that started in the past and continue to the present or have a result in the present.
examples: I have studied SEO ranking.
We have identified the problem.
grammar.context_sentence: For nearly as long as there’s been people, there’s been trade.

grammar.title: Modal Verbs of Obligation (have to)
examples: You have to get legalized.
They have to follow the rules.

grammar.title: Conditional Sentences (Type 1)
grammar.explanation: Used to talk about possible situations and their likely results.
grammar.structure: If + present simple, will + base verb
examples: If you study SEO, you will rank higher.
If you find the right product, you will succeed.
grammar.context_sentence: if you just remember I told you that international trade is all about credibility

grammar.title: Relative Clauses
grammar.explanation: Used to provide additional information about a noun.
examples: The product which you can internationally trade.
The company that handles distribution.
grammar.context_sentence: Once you have a product which you can internationally trade, its time for you to find a supplier or a local manufacturer that can make the product

grammar.title: Gerunds and Infinitives
grammar.explanation: Used to express the purpose or reason for an action.
examples: In order to get any certification.
Starting a business in the international trade field.
grammar.context_sentence: in order to get any certification any license to make your activities legalized

grammar.title: Adverbial Clauses of Purpose
grammar.explanation: Used to explain the reason for an action.
examples: In order to find your product.
In order to move your cargo.
grammar.context_sentence: in order to find your product and also do your market research

contents.title: Writing a Basic Form - Web Development

vocabulary.definition: a pre-designed document or file that can be used as a starting point for creating new documents or files
examples: The company uses a standard template for all its reports.
I created a template for my resume to make it easier to update.
vocabulary.context_sentence: talking about how to use templates.

vocabulary.definition: a piece of software designed for a specific purpose
examples: This is a web application.
I downloaded a new application for my phone.
vocabulary.context_sentence: to build a few templates in a simple web application.

vocabulary.definition: a function or method that processes a specific request or event in a program
examples: The event handler responds to user clicks.
The handler processes the data from the form.
vocabulary.context_sentence: It's got one handler on slash called main page.

vocabulary.definition: to receive or derive a characteristic or property from a parent class or object
examples: The new class inherits properties from the parent class.
She inherited her love of music from her mother.
vocabulary.context_sentence: I've made my main page handler inherit from a class called Handler, which I defined up here.

vocabulary.definition: a function associated with an object or class
examples: The class has several methods.
The method calculates the average.
vocabulary.context_sentence: this just adds one method called Write.

vocabulary.definition: functions designed to assist other functions or processes, often simplifying complex tasks
examples: We'll create helper functions to handle data validation.
The code uses helper functions to improve readability.
vocabulary.context_sentence: And we'll be adding a few more helper functions, to this handler, as we go.

vocabulary.definition: a text-based interface used to interact with a computer system
examples: I opened the terminal to run the command.
The developer used the terminal to debug the code.
vocabulary.context_sentence: Let's go ahead, and make sure this works. We go to our terminal.

vocabulary.definition: a location on a computer's file system where files and other directories are stored
examples: I created a new directory to organize my files.
The file is located in the root directory.
vocabulary.context_sentence: I'm in the template lesson directory, where these files are.

vocabulary.definition: a value that is passed to a function or method
examples: The function takes two parameters.
The parameter controls the size of the image.
vocabulary.context_sentence: it'll just add that parameter to the URL.

vocabulary.definition: to send a form or data to a server for processing
examples: Please submit the form.
I submitted my application online.
vocabulary.context_sentence: Which means when I submit this form, it'll just add that parameter to the URL.

vocabulary.definition: the rules and structure of a programming language
examples: The code has a syntax error.
Understanding the syntax is crucial for writing code.
vocabulary.context_sentence: This is basically the python syntax for a multi line string.

vocabulary.definition: a string that spans multiple lines in code
examples: Use a multi-line string for long text.
The code uses a multi-line string for HTML.
vocabulary.context_sentence: This is basically the python syntax for a multi line string.

vocabulary.definition: to remember something
examples: I recall the incident clearly.
Can you recall what happened last night?
vocabulary.context_sentence: Now, recall that since we didn't set a method on that form, the default method is going to be GET.

grammar.title: Present Simple for habitual actions and facts
grammar.explanation: Used to describe habits, general truths, repeated actions, or unchanging situations.
examples: I go to the gym every day.
The sun rises in the east.
grammar.context_sentence: It's got one handler on slash called main page.

grammar.title: Use of 'which' for non-defining relative clauses
grammar.explanation: Used to add extra information about a noun in the main clause. The information is not essential to identify the noun.
examples: I went to London, which is a beautiful city.
He failed the exam, which was disappointing.

grammar.title: Use of 'as' to show time or reason
grammar.explanation: Used to indicate that something happens at the same time as something else or to give a reason.
examples: As I was walking home, it started to rain.
As it was raining, we stayed inside.
grammar.context_sentence: As you can see, ,we'll run the app server, like so I, I prefer to run

grammar.title: Conditional Sentences (Type 1)
grammar.explanation: Used to talk about possible situations and their likely results in the future.
grammar.structure: If + present simple, will + base form
examples: If it rains, I will stay home.
If you study hard, you will pass the exam.
grammar.context_sentence: If you want to make a basic form we can just return html directly from our handler here.

grammar.title: Use of 'going to' for future plans
grammar.explanation: Used to express intentions, plans, or predictions based on present evidence.
examples: I am going to visit my family next week.
They are going to start a new project.
grammar.context_sentence: Now in this slide, I'm going to ask you lots of questions like, what happens when you do x or y or whatever.

grammar.title: Use of 'since' to show time
grammar.explanation: Used to indicate a period of time that started in the past and continues to the present.
examples: I have lived here since 2010.
She has been working here since Monday.
grammar.context_sentence: Now, recall that since we didn't set a method on that form, the default method is going to be GET.
